25 - Section 10.2.mp4
All right, now that you understand market leader content, you have to then put it somewhere to externally evangelize it for you and the best place to do that is your website. I call them magnetic websites, right? I want to bring in— and evangelize how I'm the market leader. And let my sight do all the selling. So by the time I talk to a prospect, we're not asking things like testimonials, are we good, or who we are, or what proof do we have— all the things we've covered in our one-page attack and conquer plan. We're literally just talking about timing and pricing. I want that same thing for you. So let's give you some examples. These are actual clients of mine. Um... So let's take a look at the first one.
Okay, it's a little distorted here because of the size of the screen. But immediately you hit it here. Compelling content. This is a private jet company. You've got good video. You've got their... their tagline: 'Get there faster.' Verity Jet Group is the authority in light and mid-size aircraft transactions. Right, so that's their niche. There's only two calls to action: Buy your jet or sell your jet. And I want to be clear—whatever the objective is, you always want, whatever the objective is, you want to fully put it out there, right? So, if you're trying to drill them into a credibility asset or you're trying to... capture a lead, be upfront with that. Then you scroll down. They've got a great video. Back to that video content.
I'm not going to play it. But it walks through who they are, what they do, what proof they have, what assets they have, and why they're the market leader. Then it's followed up here with 500 million plus in successful transactions, 30% on average faster transactions, 25 plus years of experience, credibility asset, credibility asset, credibility asset. Which is reinforcing up here why they're the authority and light midsize aircraft. So, hopefully, you can see what we're doing there. Boom, our difference. Core values, superior market insight, speed, whatever it takes, customer first, right? So they're bringing their core values as a differentiator on their homepage. Right. Boom. Reinforcing it with client testimonials all through here. Right, and then ending the page with an authoritative piece of content.
That they've built a mini book on taking the guesswork out of buying a jet. Right. This is all on one scroll of a page. Boom. I clearly identified my niche. I'm showing you my credibility assets. I'm using my core values as a differentiator. I'm validating that clients want to work with us and that everything I just told you is real. And then I've created an authoritative piece of content. And then I'm taking action right here. Make sense? Okay, here's another example of that market leader content to create a magnetic website. So you've got GTN as the leader in technical staffer gene leveraging. Innovation to drive next-generation recruiting to Fortune 2000 companies— two options here: find candidates or search careers. Scroll down. It talks about who they are, what they do, the niches that they're in.
Um, 385 uh satisfied clients, 3, 500 jobs filled, eight Inc. 5,000 awards, 15 countries in global reach, credibility asset, credibility asset, credibility asset. Scroll down. Clearly defines what they do. And then they've created sub-niche brands, right? So they're the leaders in cyber, digital, payroll, and professional services, all within staffing. You keep scrolling. GTN difference, core values, core values. They're selling this as a differentiator to work there and to work with them. Then they have Google reviews getting pulled in, praise for our tech staff and recruiting services. Someone else is selling credibility asset, credibility asset, credibility asset. And you keep going. Now we're down to our results, client testimonials. Call to action. So again, these are just a couple of examples.
We'll drop some more in here, but the idea is: how do you take now your one-page attack and conquer? Plan, who you are, what you do, what proof you have, why people want it, your core values, your belief system, and start to now. Evangelize and externally bring that to life on the website— why you're the market leader, why you're in this niche, why everybody wants to work with you, what the proof is. You want, when you get the opportunity to get a lead, you remove the selling and you go straight into the closing because you've qualified it through market leader content and magnetic websites. See you in the Q &A.
